title: NOC 21110 – Biologists and Related Scientists | GTR Immigration
description: Biologists, microbiologists, geneticists, toxicologists, and related life-science professionals play a critical role in environmental protection, biotechnology, healthcare innovation, and pharmaceutical research across Canada. Classified under NOC 21110 (TEER 1), this highly skilled occupation continues to experience growing nationwide demand—creating excellent immigration prospects through Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), Express Entry–linked pathways, and employer-supported work permits.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree

employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in biology or related discipline
Master’s or PhD for research-scientist roles
Professional regulation in Alberta and British Columbia
job_outlook: From 2022–2031, Canada is projected to generate approximately 11,500 new roles in this field, while about 9,600 job seekers are expected—indicating favourable prospects for internationally trained life-science professionals pursuing PR strategies.
additional_information: Biologists and Related Scientists are increasingly recruited across government agencies, research institutions, hospitals, biotechnology companies, and environmental consulting firms. Demand has accelerated since the pandemic and is forecast to remain strong through the next decade.

Federal and provincial government laboratoriesEnvironmental consulting firmsPharmaceutical and biotech companiesUniversities and research institutesUtilities and natural-resource companiesHospitals and medical research centres
Annual salaries typically range between $46,200 and $113,500 CAD, depending on specialization, education level, and province.
Why Choose Canada in This Occupation?
Immigrating as a life-science professional offers:
